text.title: enum-constraints-closed-vocabularies Decision Tree
text.summary: Choose enum, semi-open union, or free string based on value-set shape.

decision-tree.root-question: Is the set of valid values finite AND known at schema-definition time?

branch.conclusion: Use plain `str` WITHOUT a pattern (or with a description-only hint). Enum would clip valid answers.

branch.question: Is the set fully closed (no novel values ever expected)?
Does the decoder support strict mode or a grammar backend?

branch.conclusion: Use `Literal[...]` or `enum`. Decoder masks all non-enum tokens; no further work.
Use `Literal[...]` for documentation; add a post-validation retry-with-feedback loop or a deterministic mapping table for drift.
Use a discriminated union with `known` (Literal[...]) + `novel` (free str) branches. NEVER `Literal | str`.

text.example: q-finite → branch(true) → q-fully-closed → branch(true) → q-decoder-strict → branch(true) → conclusion(r1-literal-or-enum).
